description_long: This object is a stylized blue flower with six broad, rounded petals radiating symmetrically from a central disc. The petals are slightly curved and overlap gently at their bases, creating a whorled arrangement around the center. The central disc is lighter in color, almost white, and slightly raised compared to the petals. The petals have a smooth, matte finish with subtle gradients of blue and hints of purple near the center. There are no visible leaves or stem attached to the flower, and the underside is not visible in any view. The overall form is low and wide, resembling a decorative or ornamental flower often found in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A stylized blue flower with six broad, overlapping petals arranged symmetrically around a pale central disc. The petals are smooth and gently curved, giving the flower a soft, ornamental appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full spread of six blue petals radiating from a pale center, with the petals appearing broad and slightly overlapping.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the gentle curvature and layering of the petals, with the central disc more visible and the petal edges slightly foreshortened.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this view shows the symmetrical arrangement of the petals and the even distribution around the center.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, again emphasizing the smooth, curved petals and the central disc, with subtle shading differences.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration, with each petal symmetrically spaced around the central disc and slightly overlapping at the base. The arrangement is regular and exhibits clear radial symmetry.
